meta.title: Web Loading Strategies – Lazy, Eager, Async & Defer (2026)
meta.description: Learn different web loading strategies like lazy loading, eager loading, async and defer to improve website performance and speed.

content.value: Loading strategies define how and when resources like images, scripts, and styles are loaded in a webpage. Choosing the right strategy improves performance and user experience.
Efficient loading reduces initial page load time, improves Core Web Vitals, and ensures faster rendering of important content.

rows: Delays script execution until HTML parsing is complete

content.value: Lazy loading delays loading of non-critical resources until they are needed, such as when they enter the viewport.
Eager loading loads important resources immediately during page load.
The async attribute allows scripts to load independently without blocking HTML parsing.
The defer attribute delays script execution until the HTML document is fully parsed.

value: **Use lazy loading for images and videos**

content.value: Using the right loading strategies significantly improves website performance. Combining lazy loading, async, and defer ensures faster load times and better user experience.

faq.question: What is lazy loading?
faq.answer: It delays loading of non-critical resources until needed.

faq.question: What is the difference between async and defer?
faq.answer: Async executes immediately after loading, while defer waits until HTML parsing is complete.

faq.question: When should I use eager loading?
faq.answer: For critical content like hero images.

quiz.question: Which attribute loads scripts without blocking?
Which is best for critical images?
